name: DORIAN LPG LTD.
business: Dorian LPG Ltd. is engaged in the transportation of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) using very large gas carriers (VLGCs). It operates a fleet of twenty-seven VLGCs, including time chartered-in vessels, with an aggregate carrying capacity of approximately 2.3 million cbm. The company generates the majority of its revenues through the Helios Pool, a joint venture that commercially manages VLGCs and distributes net pool revenues to participants.
namedCustomers: China International United Petroleum & Chemicals Co., Ltd.

exposures.sentence: Specifically, Dorian and its subsidiaries are focused on owning and operating VLGCs in the LPG shipping industry.
We are engaged in the transportation of LPG.
Two of our vessels are capable of fully loading ammonia cargoes, and three of our vessels have been fitted to be able to load up to 87% of their cargo capacity with ammonia.

catalysts.event: Prepayment of $16.5 million on 2023 A&R Debt Facility related to VLGC Cobra
catalysts.sentence: On April 20, 2026, we prepaid $16.5 million of the 2023 A&R Debt Facility, a proportion related to the 2015-built VLGC Cobra.

catalysts.event: Sale of VLGC Cobra, receiving net proceeds of $81.9 million
catalysts.sentence: On May, 6, 2026, we completed the sale of this vessel, receiving proceeds net of commission and fees of $81.9 million.

catalysts.event: Declaration of irregular cash dividend of $1.00 per share
catalysts.sentence: On May 7, 2026, we announced that our Board of Directors has declared an irregular cash dividend of $1.00 per share of the Company's common stock totaling $42.8 million.

tamClaims.claim: As of May 22, 2026, the world VLGC fleet consisted of 427 vessels with aggregate carrying capacity of 35.8 million cbm, with 124 vessels (including 78 ammonia carriers) on order for delivery by end of 2029, representing 11.1 million cbm of capacity.
tamClaims.sentence: According to industry sources, in the VLGC sector in which we operate as of May 22, 2026, there were 427 vessels with an aggregate carrying capacity of 35.8 million cbm in the world fleet and 124 vessels (including 78 ammonia carriers) with 11.1 million cbm of capacity on order for delivery by the end of 2029.
